history.content:
<thought>
The agent is facing forward at cell D7. In front of the agent (cell D6) is lava, so moving forward is unsafe. Turning right would face the agent toward cell E7, which is empty and safe to move into. I will TURN_RIGHT to move around the lava and progress safely.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing forward at column D row 7. The cell ahead (D6) is floor (.), so it is safe to move forward. I should move forward to explore more and get closer to potential goal areas while avoiding lava.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing north with a wall (#) right in front of me at D6, so I cannot move forward. I should consider turning to find a path. To my left at C7 is lava (L), which is deadly, so turning left is unsafe. To my right at E7 is a wall (#), so turning right won't help either. The best choice is to turn around by taking two consecutive turns. I will start by turning left to orient west and then reassess.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is at row 07, column D, facing upward. Directly ahead (row 06, column D) is lava, which is unsafe. To move safely, turning left or right might provide a safer path. Checking the adjacent cells at the agent's level: to the right (column E) and left (column C) are floor cells. Turning right will face the agent towards an open floor cell in column E, row 7. I will TURN_RIGHT to face that direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am currently facing forward towards column D row 7. The cell directly in front of me at D7 is a wall ('#'), so I cannot move forward. To find a path forward towards potentially safe and open areas, I should turn to face a direction where the cells are accessible. Looking at the map, to my left is a floor cell (C7 '.', safe) and to my right is a wall (E7 '#'). Thus, I will turn left to face the open space at C7.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am located at row 07 column D facing upwards. The cell right in front of me at row 06 column D is lava, which is deadly. I can see that the path to the left at column C is a floor cell, which is safe. To avoid lava and move to a safe cell, I will turn left and then move forward in subsequent steps.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The agent is currently facing upwards and is located at row 07, column D. The cell directly in front is a wall (#), which cannot be passed. To find a path forward, the agent should turn to change direction and explore other options. Turning right will face the agent to the right, where the cell is lava (L), so it is unsafe to move forward there. Turning left will face the agent to the left, which is a wall. Turning left is no better, turning right is unsafe, so the agent should turn left to avoid facing lava.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The area ahead appears clear with no visible immediate obstacles or lava. The map shows walls mainly to the left and right sides but not directly in front of me. Given the goal is not yet visible, moving forward to explore more of the map is the best step to proceed and possibly find the goal in upcoming views.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I see that I am on row 07, column D, facing forward towards row 06, column D which is a wall (#). I cannot move forward because of the wall directly ahead. I should turn to face a path where I can move safely. Turning left will face me towards column C where there are open floor cells. Therefore, I will turn left to explore a safe path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am currently facing forward at the cell in the same column D, row 6. The cell directly ahead looks like a floor cell, so it is safe to move forward. Moving forward is the best choice for now to keep progressing.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
I am facing forward at D7, which is an empty floor cell. However, there are lava cells to my left and right, so I want to be careful. The path straight ahead seems clear at D6 and D5. Moving forward keeps me in a safe corridor with no immediate lava. Continuing forward is the best option to explore and find the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing forward at D7, with a wall (#) right in front at D6. The immediate front cell is a wall, so moving forward is not possible. The left cell at C7 is lava (L), which is dangerous. The right cell at E7 is a wall (#), so not traversable. Turning to the right would face a wall; turning left would face lava. Since turning left faces a lava cell and turning right faces a wall, turning left to eventually find a path might be safer and exploring that direction could reveal new space to navigate.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The goal is visible at the top row, column G, which is far ahead and to my right. The path directly ahead seems to be free floor cells, so it is safe to move forward. I should move forward to approach the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>
